META_DOCUMENTATION.plan_type_rationale: Multi-task post-merge validation across test suite, RAG embeddings, and analysis-skill spot-checks. Hard stops after data-changing operations and at final review. Predominantly read-only.
META_DOCUMENTATION.objective: Verify that the merged populate-relationships+headers fix (origin/main = fe6bdc9) does not regress downstream consumers: full test suite, RAG embeddings, three analysis skills, and committed snapshots.

phase_1.title: Baseline test re-run on origin/main
phase_1.purpose: Confirm the post-merge state of CODEREF-CORE matches what we observed during the merge sequence: 1274 pass / 66 fail. Establish the validation baseline.

tasks.task: cd C:/Users/willh/Desktop/CODEREF/CODEREF-CORE && git status — confirm working tree state. Expected: clean wrt source files; coderef/working/ and coderef/archived/ untracked is OK.
git log -1 --oneline — confirm HEAD is fe6bdc9 (post-merge state) on main.
cd C:/Users/willh/Desktop/CODEREF/CODEREF-CORE && npm test 2>&1 | tail -30 — full suite, not focused. Capture the final summary line.

Compare against expected baseline: Test Files = 21 failed | 57 passed (78); Tests = 66 failed | 1274 passed (1340). If the actuals match within tolerance (±2 tests for flake), proceed. If actuals show MORE failures than baseline, halt and surface what regressed.
Append baseline numbers to communication.json.communication_log so the validation has a recorded ground truth.

success_criteria: Pass/fail counts match post-merge baseline within tolerance

phase_2.title: Sample post-fix scanner output
phase_2.purpose: Confirm the merged fix is producing real relationship data in .coderef/index.json (not just passing tests on test fixtures).

tasks.task: Confirm .coderef/index.json exists in CODEREF-CORE and is post-merge (mtime > 2026-05-01T17:00 UTC). If missing or stale, run: cd CODEREF-CORE && /coderef-pipeline coderef-core --stages=populate to regenerate.

Sample one element with rich relationships: cd CODEREF-CORE && python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('.coderef/index.json')); cands=[x for x in d['elements'] if x.get('imports') and x.get('usedBy')]; print('total elements:', len(d['elements']), 'with imports:', sum(1 for x in d['elements'] if x.get('imports')), 'with usedBy:', sum(1 for x in d['elements'] if x.get('usedBy'))); print('sample:'); print(json.dumps(cands[0], indent=2) if cands else '<<no candidate>>')"

Expected from the merged fix's verification: ~2283 elements, ~2053 with imports, ~1486 with usedBy. The sampled element should have non-empty imports[] and usedBy[] arrays with structured objects (not strings).
Confirm .coderef/semantic-registry.json exists, mtime matches index.json, and reports generated_from = '.coderef/index.json' in its metadata.
Append the sampled element JSON to communication.json.communication_log as evidence.

success_criteria: index.json contains rich relationship data on the expected scale
Sample element has populated imports[] and usedBy[] arrays

phase_3.title: RAG re-index against post-fix scanner output
phase_3.purpose: Refresh RAG vector embeddings so they reflect the new richer scanner output. Closes the silent-quality-regression risk identified in the issue investigator deep-dive.

tasks.task: /coderef-rag-server status — verify the RAG server on port 52849 is reachable. Earlier in the session, several services failed (Lloyd routers, OPENCLAW Monitoring API, watcher); RAG server status not directly confirmed.
If RAG server is down: /coderef-rag-server start. Wait for health endpoint OK before proceeding.
Capture pre-reindex vector index timestamp. Path candidates: .coderef/coderef-vectors.json, .coderef/rag-index.json. ls -l <path> and record the mtime.
/coderef-rag-server index coderef-core OR cd CODEREF-CORE && node dist/src/cli/rag-index.js — invoke the canonical reindex path. May take several minutes.
Verify post-reindex vector index timestamp is later than the pre-reindex timestamp. If unchanged, the reindex did not actually run; halt and investigate.
/coderef-rag-server status — confirm server is still healthy after reindex (memory pressure during reindex sometimes crashes the server).
Append pre/post timestamps and reindex duration to communication_log.

success_criteria: Vector index file timestamp updated post-reindex
RAG server health endpoint OK after reindex
phase_3.hard_stop_reason: Reindex is the only file-modifying step in this workorder (writes new vector data). Operator confirms it completed cleanly before proceeding to skill spot-checks.

phase_4.title: Spot-check analyze-coderef-semantics
phase_4.purpose: First analysis skill exercise. Most directly affected by --source-headers writing real headers for the first time post-merge.

tasks.task: Invoke /analyze-coderef-semantics against the project. Capture stdout/stderr and final report.
Verify the skill completes without throwing an unhandled exception. A graceful 'no headers found' or 'partial coverage' result is acceptable; a stack trace is not.
If the skill produces a report file (e.g., .coderef/reports/semantic-headers.json or similar), verify it parses as JSON and contains element entries.
Append skill outcome (passed / failed / report-path) to communication_log.

success_criteria: Report (if any) is valid JSON or markdown

phase_5.title: Spot-check coderef-analyze
phase_5.purpose: Top-level wrapper. Surfaces aggregate behavior change quickly across multiple sub-checks.

tasks.task: Invoke /coderef-analyze against the project. Capture stdout/stderr and final report.
Verify the skill completes without throwing. Allow individual sub-checks to report findings; reject only if the wrapper itself crashes.
Append skill outcome to communication_log.

success_criteria: Wrapper completes; individual sub-check findings recorded but do not block phase pass

phase_6.title: Spot-check data-source-report-use-case-analyzer
phase_6.purpose: Heaviest semantic-registry consumer. If anything is going to mis-handle the new richer relationship data, this is most likely to surface it.

tasks.task: Invoke /data-source-report-use-case-analyzer against the project. Capture stdout/stderr and final report.
Verify the skill consumes the populated semantic-registry.json without errors.
Append skill outcome to communication_log.

phase_7.title: Snapshot review + selective regen
phase_7.purpose: If any committed __snapshots__ pin index.json or semantic-registry.json contents, they will diff post-merge. Regenerate ONLY if user confirms diffs are intentional.

tasks.task: find CODEREF-CORE -path '*/__snapshots__/*.snap' 2>&1 | head -20 — list snapshot files.
grep -l 'index.json\|semantic-registry.json' <each snapshot file> — identify which snapshots reference scanner output.

For each affected snapshot, sample the diff against current scanner output. Determine if differences are entirely additions of relationship data (expected) or include changes to existing fields (unexpected).
Surface diff to user for confirmation. Do NOT auto-regenerate. If user confirms intentional, proceed; otherwise halt and re-investigate.
If approved: cd CODEREF-CORE && npx vitest run --update-snapshot <scoped-test-paths> — regenerate only the affected snapshots.
Re-run the full vitest suite to confirm snapshot regen didn't introduce failures elsewhere.
Append snapshot regen outcome (paths, line-count delta, post-regen test result) to communication_log.

success_criteria: Regen happens only after user confirmation
Post-regen test suite still at baseline

phase_8.title: Final review + closure
phase_8.purpose: Aggregate validation findings into a closure summary. Decide: clean pass, conditional pass with follow-up stubs, or fail-and-rollback.

tasks.task: Re-read communication.json.communication_log: confirm baseline test counts, RAG reindex timestamps, three skill outcomes, and snapshot regen status are all recorded.

Tally results. Clean pass = baseline tests match + RAG reindexed + three skills clean + snapshots either unchanged or regen-confirmed. Conditional pass = clean tests + skills work + 1 known issue surfaced and stubbed. Fail = test regressions OR skill crash OR snapshot diff that's not just additions.
Author final summary in communication_log with verdict and any follow-up stub IDs needed.
Surface verdict to user for closure approval before /close-workorder runs.

success_criteria: All phase outcomes recorded in communication_log
phase_8.hard_stop_reason: Final approval before /close-workorder. Validation results may inform whether STUB-241's stated regressions need follow-up workorders before closure.

execution_rules.atomicity: Read-only validation. No commits. No code changes. The only file-modifying steps are RAG reindex (Phase 3) and snapshot regen (Phase 7); both are gated by hard stops.
execution_rules.rollback: If any validation step surfaces a regression: HALT, record in communication_log, do NOT modify code or snapshots, surface to user. User decides between authoring a fix workorder (out of scope) or accepting the regression as a known issue (with separate stub).
execution_rules.service_management: Phase 3 requires the RAG server (port 52849) to be reachable. If it died from the cascade failure earlier in the session, restart it before proceeding.

hard_constraints: Read-only validation — no source code changes inside this workorder
Snapshot regen requires explicit user confirmation
If full test suite shows MORE failures than the post-merge baseline of 66, HALT and author a fix workorder
Do not touch OPENCLAW → automation rename — that work is tracked separately and out of scope here
acceptance_criteria: Full vitest suite executed; pass/fail count within tolerance of post-merge baseline (1274 pass / 66 fail)
.coderef/index.json contains rich relationship data on the expected scale (~2283 elements, ~2053 with imports, ~1486 with usedBy)
RAG vector index file timestamp updated post-reindex
RAG server health OK after reindex
Any committed scanner-output snapshots either match current output or are explicitly regenerated with user approval
Final verdict (pass / conditional pass / fail) recorded in communication_log
